# Migrate discussions data to hierarchical chunking (XXxx)

### Context
Per the Epic #11120, we are applying the hierarchical chunking (`XXxx/`) storage pattern to GitHub content directories. The code enablement for Discussions has been handled in #11118. 

### The Problem
While `resources/content/discussions/` is currently well below the 1,000-file GitHub ceiling, we are moving to symmetrically chunk all GH-content directories. We are separating this data migration from the code logic (per #11116) to prevent file-move diffs from obscuring logic review.

### The Architectural Reality
The chunking algorithm (`id.slice(0, -2) + 'xx'`) natively distributes these files into 100-file increments without breaking ingestion paths, now that the underlying code supports it.

### The Fix
- Physically move all `.md` files currently in `resources/content/discussions/` and its corresponding archive into the newly required `XXxx/` nested directory structure.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% of Discussion markdown files are located in `XXxx` subdirectories.
- [ ] No flat `.md` files remain at the root of `resources/content/discussions/` (excluding index/readme files if any).
- [ ] Data-only PR is opened and cleanly passes review without logic changes.
